notes: This is the mission-level Observatory record. The record's own Contacts name only Joe Zender (PI). Strong candidates were drawn from the SWAP/LYRA Instrument SPASE records and the official ESA PROBA2 mission team page.

The Observatory XML did NOT contain InstrumentID references; SWAP and LYRA Instrument records were located directly at spase-metadata.org/SMWG/Instrument/PROBA2/. Instruments DSLP and TPMU are mentioned in the ResourceHeader but no SPASE Instrument records were confirmed; their PIs (Travnicek, Hruska) come from the ESA team page only.

Mission description paper selected per heuristic: 'PROBA2: Mission and Spacecraft Overview', Santandrea et al. 2013, Solar Physics 286:5-19 (2013SoPh..286....5S, DOI 10.1007/s11207-013-0289-5) — multi-author mission overview published at the start of operations (launch 2009-11-02). Crossref author order: S. Santandrea (lead), K. Gantois, K. Strauch, F. Teston, E. Tilmans, C. Baijot, D. Gerrits, A. De Groof, G. Schwehm, J. Zender (plus corporate authors 'PROBA2 Project Team' and 'PROBA2 Industry Team').

Instrument PIs (Berghmans, Dominique, Defise, Schmutz) and the other instrument PIs are listed here because they are named PIs on the official ESA mission team page (Strong). The role-assignment downstream agent should decide whether instrument-level PIs belong in the Observatory authorship or only in their respective Instrument records.
Crossref returned no ORCIDs or affiliations for the overview paper; affiliations shown come from the ESA team page. ORCIDs were not separately looked up (out of scope).
'P. Travenicek' on the ESA team page is almost certainly Pavel Travnicek (DSLP) — spelling differs; downstream should verify identity/spelling.

Person records confirmed: Joe.Zender, David.Berghmans, Marie.Dominique, Anik.De.Groof. No Person record found (HTTP 404) for Gerhard.Schwehm or Stefano.Santandrea; not checked for Defise, Schmutz, Travnicek, Hruska, Strauch, Gantois (downstream lookup).

Long-tail instrument-paper co-authors and support staff (Willems, D'Huys, Yalim, Reis, Hannoteau) noted but not listed individually — support/student roles alone are below the inclusion bar for the mission-level record.
